taera_intro.title: The Earth
taera_intro.narrator: Taera. Earth carrier. What she's wearing.
taera_intro.text:
The earth smells like rain the day before.

Even when it didn't rain.

She keeps the smells for a long time.

That's why I like to dig.

To find out what's left.

taera_ce_quelle_garde.title: What Keeps Earth
taera_ce_quelle_garde.text:
Everything.

The seeds we forgot to plant.
The roots of the trees we cut.
The fingerprints of the people who left.

The earth has a longer memory than men.

When I touch the ground somewhere,
I feel what happened there.

Not clear.

Like a vibration.

A house that has welcomed many people
vibrates differently from an empty house.

A battlefield vibrates differently from a garden.

My mom thought I was making up.

My brother thought I was weird.

My dad thought it was a gift.

taera_enfance.title: The Garden
taera_enfance.text:
We lived in the mountains.

Not very high.
Just enough to see the plain below.

My father was growing vegetables.

Not to sell.
To eat.
For the neighbors.
For those who were missing.

He said:

**"The earth gives to those who give it. *

I had my hands in the earth every day.

Cold in the morning.
Hot afternoon.
Humid after the rain.
Dry in summer.

I was learning his moods
before learning to read.

taera_pere.title: The Father of Taera
taera_pere.text:
My father had hands like roots.

Large.
Strong.
Covered with soil even after washing.

He didn't talk much.
But when he was talking,
It was always at the right time.

At night he sat by the garden.
He was watching what had pushed.

He didn't count.
He wasn't measuring.

He was just looking.

Once I asked him what he saw.

**"What was there before. *
**"And what will be there after. *

I didn't understand.

Now I get it.

That's exactly what I see, too.

taera_decouverte.title: The First Time
taera_decouverte.text:
I was six.

A tree fell into the storm.

Dude.
Huge.
He blocked the way to the neighbouring village.

The men were trying to move him.
Impossible.

I was there watching them.

I put my hand on the trunk.

Just to touch him.

And the tree moved.

Not much.
Just enough for everyone to stop.

The men looked at me.

I looked at my hand.

The bark was warm.
Like the tree still breathes.

taera_reaction_village.title: The Village
taera_reaction_village.text:
Bad.

Not violently.

But the eyes had changed.

The women in the village signed up to see me passing.
The kids were moving away.

My mother came by my hand.
She was smiling at others.
She was shaking my hand hard.

Both at the same time.

**"You only do this at home now. *

**"But I didn't do it on purpose—"**

**"I know. *

She tightened even harder.

**"I know. *

I realized that my mother had two fears.
Fear for me.
And scared of me.

It took me a long time to accept.

That both can exist at the same time.

taera_reaction_elle.title: Its reaction
taera_reaction_elle.text:
Curious.

Especially curious.

The tree was heavy.
I felt it.
It's like his mass is crossing my arms.

But it wasn't unpleasant.

Like lifting something important.

I put my hand back on the trunk.

Nothing.

Again.

The tree shook.

I smiled.

Then my father came.
He saw my hand on the tree.
He saw my smile.

He didn't say anything.

In the evening he taught me how to plant seeds.

Like it's the logical suite.

taera_apprendre.title: Learning Alone
taera_apprendre.text:
My father taught me how to garden.

He didn't know that was what he was doing.

But plant a seed and watch it grow —
It's exactly the same gesture as holding his sword.
and feel the earth responding.

Give before taking.
Wait until you act.
Listen before you talk.

I did this for two years.

No name.
Not understanding everything.

Just do it.

The earth does not ask questions.
She answers what we give her.

taera_limites.title: Limits
taera_limites.text:
I found one.

Only one really.

If I gave too much —
without rest,
without letting the earth regenerate —

I was falling apart.

Not physically.

Inside.

Like a field overplanted.

After too much effort,
I slept two days.
Hands stuck to the ground.
Like I need to be recharged.

My brother found me there in the morning.

He said nothing.
He was laying a blanket on me.
And he was gonna do the rest of the work.

That's how we communicated.

Him and me.

No words.

taera_recrutement.title: Peter's Woman
taera_recrutement.text:
She arrived one autumn morning.

A woman.

Great.
Dressed in brown and green.

She felt the forest after the rain.

She stopped in front of our garden.

She looked at the earth.

Then she looked at me.

**"You talk to the earth. *

**"She answers me. *

She seemed satisfied.

**"How long? *

**"Since I was six. *

**"And you were not afraid. *

**"No. *

**"Why? *

I was thinking.

**"Because the earth does not hurt people who listen to it. *

She smiled.

**"True. *

taera_femme_peur.title: Not Fear
taera_femme_peur.text:
No.

She felt the forest.

People who smell the forest
usually do no harm.

It's a rule I set alone.

Probably not very reliable.

But she'd been here.

What surprised me was her look.

She looked at me like she knew something.
I didn't know yet.

No pity.
No mistrust.

Just: I know where you're going. *

It was both reassuring and terrifying.

taera_depart_famille.title: Departure
taera_depart_famille.text:
My father listened to the stone woman.

Long time.

My mother was crying softly in the kitchen.

My brother was standing against the wall.
Cross arms.
Like Ikar now that I think about it.

In the end my father got up.

He went out into the garden.

I followed him.

He took a handful of dirt.

He put it in my hand.

**"Will you keep it? *

**"Yes. *

**"Good. *

He's back.

I put the earth in my palm.

I still have it.
In a small purse.
Around the neck.

taera_frere.title: Brother
taera_frere.text:
He didn't say anything about the whole conversation.

When I went out with my bag,
He was on the doorstep.

He gave me something.

A little stone.
Round.
Grey.
Smooth as if she had spent years in a river.

**"It comes from the garden. *

**"I know. *

**"She was under the tree you moved when you were six. *

I looked at him.

**"Remember? *

**"I was always watching when you were doing weird things. *

He had raised his shoulders.

**"To see if it was dangerous. *

**"And? *

**"No. *

He was home.

I put the stone in the purse with the earth.

She's still there.

taera_aurenthal.title: Auenthal
taera_aurenthal.text:
Aurenthal felt the stone and the crowd.

Not unpleasant.

Just different.

The Temple smelled old wood and incense.

The other three were waiting.

The girl with silver hair looked at her hands.

The forearm burn boy said:
You're late. *

Not mine.
The silver girl.

I smiled at him anyway.

He didn't smile in return.

The other boy — his eyes everywhere — had made me a sign.

*"My name is Vael. *

**"Taera. *

**"How long did you travel? *

**"Three days. *

*"I five. *
**"The mountains are far away. *

He knew where I came from without me saying it.

I thought it was nice.

taera_ikar_debut.title: Ikar — The First Day
taera_ikar_debut.text:
He was difficult.

Not bad.

Hard.

He took too much space in conversations.
He decided before we finished talking.
He was acting before we finished thinking.

The second week I put bread in front of him.

He looked up.

**"You don't eat in the morning. *

**"How do you know? *

**"The earth knows when someone is empty. *

He looked at the bread.
Then me.
Then the bread.

He ate without saying anything.

The next day there was wood in front of my room.

For the nightlight.

He didn't tell me why.

That was his way of saying thank you.

We learned to talk like that.
No words.
By gestures.

Not that different from my brother.

taera_entrainement.title: Training
taera_entrainement.text:
The Earth's spirit was called Mael.

Old.
Very old.
She smelled of humus and wet stone.

She taught me when I did.

Not by talking.

We were planting together.
We were digging together.
We were lifting together.

One day she dropped a huge rock.

**"Relevant. *

I raised it.

**« Higher. *

**"I can't. *

You didn't try. *

I tried.

I raised it higher.

**" Again. *

**"I will fall—"**

**"Then fall. *

I fell.

**"Good.** she said.
**"Now you know how far you can go. *

The land had cushioned my fall.

Sure.

choices.text: She was talking to you about the others?

taera_mael_autres.title: Mael and Others
taera_mael_autres.text:
Sometimes.

She spoke of them as elements.

**"The fire is impatient. *
**"Water is patient. *
**"The air is elusive. *

**"What about the earth? *

**"The earth is present. *

**"That's it? *

**"That's a lot. *

She planted a seed.

**"Be present. *
**"Completely there. *
**"Not in yesterday. *
**"Not tomorrow. *
**"There. *

**"This is your role in this group. *

**"To be there? *

**"To be there when others can no longer be there. *

I didn't get it at the moment.

Now I understand perfectly.

taera_pret_a_tout.title: Ready for All
taera_pret_a_tout.location: Temple of the Order — Common Hall
taera_pret_a_tout.text:
It was our third year.

Mael had brought us together.

She said:

**"The Chaos bearer will come. *
**"You will have to be ready for everything. *

Ikar said yes without hesitation.

Aryn looked at his hands.

Vael pretended to look elsewhere.

I said:

**"No. *

Everyone turned around.

Mael looked at me.

**"No what? *

**"No, I won't be ready for anything. *
**"There is always another way. *
**"We're looking for it long enough or we're not looking enough. *

Quiet.

Mael had no head.

**"Good. *

Ikar said:

**"Well how? *

She didn't answer.

taera_pourquoi_non.title: Why No
taera_pourquoi_non.text:
Because the earth does not destroy what it can feed.

It's her nature.

It can crush.
She can bury.
She can block.

But she's not burning.
She doesn't cut.
She doesn't kill if she can do otherwise.

I'm the carrier of the Earth.

My nature is to find another way.

Even if it takes longer.
Even if it's harder.
Even if everyone thinks it's impossible.

The seed in the stone.

Everyone thinks it's impossible.

The seed knows it's not.

taera_ikar_apres.title: Ikar After
taera_ikar_apres.text:
He caught me in the hallway.

You're naive. *

**"Perhaps. *

**"There is not always another way. *

**"I think so. *

**"What if you're wrong? *

I turned around.

**"Then I will be the first to admit it. *
**"And you will do what you have to do. *

He didn't say anything.

**"That's why we're many. "** I added.
**"You can do what I can't. *
**"And I can try what you forbid. *

He had no head.

I don't agree.

But he understood.

That was enough for me.

taera_annees.title: Years
taera_annees.text:
We were growing up.

My garden in the Temple was growing too.

I had set up a yard.

Nobody asked me.
I did.

Ikar used to come there sometimes.
He was sitting.
He said nothing.

Aryn came there when she was thinking too much.
Water and earth got along.

Vael never really came.
But sometimes in the morning I found little flowers
that the wind had brought.

That the wind had brought.

Sure.

taera_jardin_autres.title: The Garden of the Four
taera_jardin_autres.text:
The garden had become their way of being there
without having to talk.

Ikar came when he was angry.
The earth was absorbing it.

Aryn came when she doubted.
The plants had no doubt.

Vael was coming... I wasn't sure why.

One night I asked her.

**"Why are you coming here? *

**"Your garden smells good. *

**"That's it? *

He looked at the plants.

**"Here everything grows. *
**"Even fragile stuff. *

He was gone.

I got it.

Vael felt fragile sometimes.

And in my garden it was allowed.

taera_mael_vieille.title: Mael
taera_mael_vieille.text:
Spirits don't really get old.

But Mael became rarer.

Less present.

Like she's keeping her strength.

One night she stayed in the garden for a long time.

You're leaving? I asked.

**" Soon. *

**"After him? *

She knew who I meant.

**"Perhaps. *

**"What if we can't? *

She had laid her hand on the earth.

**"The earth remains. *
**"After all. *
**"Always. *

**"Is that supposed to reassure me? *

**"It's supposed to give you perspective. *

She was missing.

I planted a seed where his hand had been.

She gave a flower.
Still now.

taera_veille.title: The Signal
taera_veille.location: Temple of the Order — Garden — Night
taera_veille.text:
I felt it in the earth.

Not brutally.

Like a distant vibration.

The ground that trembles slightly.
Not an earthquake.

The signing of a huge power
somewhere west.

I was in the garden.
I had just planted the autumn seeds.

My hands were in the earth.

And the earth said to me:

Now. *

I got up.

I washed my hands.

I took my purse around my neck.

My father's land.
My brother's stone.

And I went to wake the others.

taera_reveille_ikar.title: Wake up Ikar
taera_reveille_ikar.text:
He was already up.

Sure.

The fire had warned him before me.

He was buttoning his armor.

*"You felt too. *

**"Yes. *

**"Ikar. *

**"What. *

**"We try first. *

He looked up.

**"That's what you always say. *

**"And I will say it again tonight. *

He seemed to mean something.

He closed his armor.

**"I know. *

It was the first time he said "I know"
Without questioning.

The earth shook under my feet.

Slightly.

A good sign.

taera_reveille_aryn.title: Wake up Aryn
taera_reveille_aryn.text:
His light was on.

She was looking at her pelvis.

*"You felt. *

**"Yes.** she said.
**"He is alone. *

**"You know how? *

**"Water knows. *

She looked up.

**"Taera. *

**"Yes. *

**"He's scared. *

**"I know. *

** "Ikar will"**

**"I talked to him. *

She looked at her pool again.

**"We have time to save him? *

The earth under my feet.
Always that vibration.

**"I think so. *

**"You think or you know? *

**"I think so. *

She shook her head.

That was enough for Aryn.

taera_depart.title: Departure
taera_depart.text:
Four silhouettes in the courtyard.

It was still dark.

Vael whistled.

**"Stop.** said Ikar.

**"Why? "** said Vael.

**"Because. *

**"Argument solid. *

I put my hand on the court floor.

The land of the Temple.

Cold.
Old.
Full of what had happened before us.

All generations that had left
Before us.

Some had not returned.

**"We're going.** said Ikar.

I put up my purse.

My father's land.
My brother's stone.

And we're gone.

taera_route.title: The Road
taera_route.text:
Land under horses' hoofs.

Every village we went through.

I felt it changed.

Fertile by the rivers.
Dry on the trays.
Heavy around old forests.

And the closer we got —

The stronger the vibration.

Not aggressive.

Wounded.

Like a tree we cut too much.
Still looking to grow
But in any direction.

**"You okay?** asked Vael.

**"He doesn't look mean. *

**"How do you know? *

**"The earth says when someone wants to hurt. *
**"This is different. *
**"It is someone who wants to help. *
**"And who doesn't know how. *

Vael looked at the road ahead.

**"It's worse in a sense. *

**"Yes. "** I said.
**"That's why we're not gonna stop him. *
**"We'll help him. *

taera_fumee.title: Smoke
taera_fumee.text:
We saw the smoke.

I put my hand on the floor from my horse.

Hard.
But possible.

The vibration was there.
Strong.
Painful.

But below —

Something sweeter.

Shame maybe.
Pain.

Not violence.

**"He's still here.** said Aryn.

**"He is alive. * I confirmed.

Ikar looked at the smoke.

Long time.

**"We're going.** he said.

I put up my scholarship one last time.

My father's land.
*The earth gives to those who give it. *

My brother's stone.
To see if it was dangerous. *

It wasn't dangerous.

It was painful.

It's different.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

taera_derniere_pensee.title: What she wore
taera_derniere_pensee.text:
The purse around the neck.

My father's land.
My brother's stone.
The dried flower of the garden of Mael.

And something I put in it
I didn't really decide.

A seed.

I didn't know what kind.

I picked her up in the Temple Garden.
the morning of departure.

Just a seed.

To remind me that even in places
who have lost everything —

Something can still grow.

If anyone takes the time to plant.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

fin_memoire_taera.title: End — Memory of Taera
fin_memoire_taera.text:
The memory slowly arises.

Like earth after rain.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.
